title: Review Exercise — A-Level Mathematics
text:
Solution
(a) \(2x^2+3\ln(2-x) = 0 \Rightarrow \ln(2-x) = -\dfrac{2x^2}{3} \Rightarrow 2-x = e^{-2x^2/3}\) \(\tag{M1, A1}\)
\[\boxed{x = 2-e^{-2x^2/3}, \quad k = -\tfrac{2}{3}} \tag{A1}\]
(b) \(x_{n+1} = 2-e^{-2x_n^2/3}\), \(x_0 = 1.9\):
\(x_1 \approx 1.910\), \(x_2 \approx 1.912\), \(x_3 \approx 1.912\), \(x_4 \approx 1.913\)
To justify \(\alpha = 1.913\): \(\tag{A1, A1, A1}\)
\(f(1.9125) \approx +0.007 > 0\) and \(f(1.9135) \approx -0.020 < 0\)
Sign change \(\Rightarrow 1.9125 < \alpha < 1.9135\), so \(\alpha = 1.913\) to 3 d.p. \(\blacksquare\) \(\tag{M1, A1}\)
(c) \(f'(x) = 4x - \dfrac{3}{2-x} = 0 \Rightarrow 4x(2-x) = 3 \Rightarrow 4x^2-8x+3 = 0\) \(\tag{M1, A1}\)
\[(2x-1)(2x-3) = 0 \implies \boxed{x = \tfrac{1}{2} \text{ or } x = \tfrac{3}{2}} \tag{M1, A1, A1}\]

title: Review Exercise — A-Level Mathematics
text:
Solution
(a) At \(x=3\): \(y = 9-15+2\ln 1 = -6\). Point \((3,-6)\). \(\tag{B1}\)
\[\frac{dy}{dx} = 2x-5+\frac{2}{x}. \quad \text{At } x=3: \frac{dy}{dx} = 6-5+\frac{2}{3} = \frac{5}{3} \tag{M1, A1}\]
Normal slope \(= -\dfrac{3}{5}\). Normal: \(y+6 = -\dfrac{3}{5}(x-3)\) \(\tag{M1}\)
\[5y+30 = -3x+9 \implies 3x+5y+21 = 0 \quad \blacksquare \tag{A1}\]
(b) \(\dfrac{dy}{dx} = 0\): \(2x-5+\dfrac{2}{x} = 0 \Rightarrow 2x^2-5x+2 = 0 \Rightarrow (2x-1)(x-2) = 0\) \(\tag{M1, A1}\)
\[\boxed{x = \tfrac{1}{2} \quad \text{or} \quad x = 2} \tag{A1}\]

title: 핵심개념 — Numerical Method (Iterative Method)
text:
f(x) = 0의 해를 직접 구하기 어려울 때, x = g(x) 형태로 변환하여 반복(iteration)으로 근사해를 구하는 방법.
y = x와 y = g(x)의 교점이 바로 해(solution).

title: 핵심개념 — Numerical Method (Iterative Method)
text:
1. Basic Notions

수렴 패턴



패턴
설명




Staircase
g(x)가 단조증가할 때 나타남. 수렴 속도가 더 빠름


Cobweb
g(x)가 단조감소할 때 나타남. 진동하며 수렴 또는 발산



KEY: Why is y = x assumed?

iteration이 해로 수렴하거나 무한대로 발산할 수 있음
Staircase 타입에서 더 빠르게 수렴

title: 핵심개념 — Numerical Method (Iterative Method)
text:
2. Standard Procedure (5 Steps)

Step 1 — f(x) 찾기
방정식을 f(x) = 0 형태로 정리한다.

⚠️ Most important but often missing! 문제에서 주어진 식을 그대로 쓰는 게 아니라, 반드시 f(x) = 0 형태로 변형해야 함.

예) \(x^2 - 5x - 7 = 0\) → \(f(x) = x^2 - 5x - 7\)



Step 2 — f(x)로 rough range 찾기 (\(\alpha < x < \beta\))
부호 변환(sign change) 을 보임으로써 대략적인 범위를 확인한다.
조건: \(f(\alpha) \cdot f(\beta) < 0\)

graph sketch를 그려서 확인하는 경우가 많음. 연속함수에서 \(f(\alpha)\)와 \(f(\beta)\)의 부호가 다르면 그 사이에 반드시 근이 존재 (중간값 정리).




Step 3 — x = g(x) 형태로 변환
f(x) = 0을 x = g(x) 형태로 변환한다.

TIP: 여러 g(x)가 가능할 때 — 수렴 조건 확인 필수!
수렴하는 g(x)를 선택해야 함: \[|g'(x)| < 1 \quad \text{in the interval } (\alpha, \beta)\]

\(|g'(x)| < 1\) → 수렴 (converge)
\(|g'(x)| \geq 1\) → 발산 (diverge)

여러 g(x) 중에서 반드시 이 조건을 만족하는 것을 골라야 iteration이 의미 있음.




Step 4 — Iteration 진행
초기값: \(x_0 = (\alpha + \beta) / 2\) (midpoint, 문제에서 따로 명시 없으면 중점 사용)
반복: \(x_1 = g(x_0),\; x_2 = g(x_1),\; x_3 = g(x_2), \ldots\)

언제 멈춰야 하나? (Stopping Criterion — Heuristic)
문제에서 요구하는 소수점 자리수(decimal place) 기준으로 멈춘다.







목표 정확도
멈추는 기준




1 decimal place
\(x_n\)와 \(x_{n-1}\)이 소수점 첫째 자리에서 일치할 때


2 decimal places
\(x_n\)과 \(x_{n-1}\)이 소수점 둘째 자리에서 일치할 때


n decimal places
두 연속 iteration 값이 n번째 자리에서 일치할 때




문제에서 “correct to 2 decimal places” 등으로 명시하는 경우가 대부분.





Step 5 — \((\alpha', \beta')\) 범위에서 f(x)에 대입하여 답 확인 ★★★
iteration을 통해 더 좁은 범위 \(\alpha' < x < \beta'\) 를 얻었으면, 반드시 \(\alpha', \beta'\)를 f(x)에 대입하여 부호 변환을 다시 확인해야 함.
조건: \(f(\alpha') \cdot f(\beta') < 0\)

⚠️ 이 마지막 step을 빠뜨리면 안 됨!
iteration 결과가 “진짜 근”을 포함하는 구간임을 수학적으로 정당화하는 단계. iteration 값 자체가 답이 아니라, 그 값을 경계로 하는 구간 안에 근이 있음을 f(x)로 검증해야 완성.
즉, 이 step은 단순히 계산 확인이 아니라 정확도(degree of accuracy)를 justify하는 과정.

title: 핵심개념 — Numerical Method (Iterative Method)
text:
3. KEY 요약

수치해석은 정확한 해를 주지 않는다. 항상 근사 구간을 좁혀가는 것.
전체 과정의 목적: rough interval \(\alpha < x < \beta\) → accurate interval \(\alpha' < x < \beta'\)
Step 5의 sign change 확인이 없으면 답이 완성되지 않은 것.
문제는 위 5단계 순서를 따라 풀어야 함.

title: 핵심개념 — Numerical Method (Iterative Method)
text:
4. 연습 예제 — Worked Examples
세 문제 모두 Standard 5-Step Procedure를 따라 풀이함.


i. \(x^2 - 5x - 7 = 0\)
Step 1 — f(x) 찾기
\[f(x) = x^2 - 5x - 7\]
Step 2 — rough range 찾기
정수값에서 부호 확인:



\(x\)
\(f(x)\)




6
\(36 - 30 - 7 = -1 < 0\)


7
\(49 - 35 - 7 = 7 > 0\)



\[f(6) \cdot f(7) = (-1)(7) < 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 6 < x < 7\]
(양수 근 기준. 음수 근은 \(-2 < x < -1\) 구간에 존재.)
Step 3 — x = g(x) 찾기
후보 1: \(x = \dfrac{x^2 - 7}{5}\) → \(g'(x) = \dfrac{2x}{5}\), \(g'(6) = 2.4 > 1\) ✗ 발산
후보 2: \(x = \sqrt{5x + 7}\) → \(g'(x) = \dfrac{5}{2\sqrt{5x+7}}\), \(g'(6) \approx 0.411 < 1\) ✓
따라서 \(g(x) = \sqrt{5x + 7}\) 사용.
Step 4 — Iteration (초기값 \(x_0 = 6.5\))
\[x_1 = \sqrt{39.5} \approx 6.285, \quad x_2 \approx 6.199, \quad x_3 \approx 6.164\] \[x_4 \approx 6.150, \quad x_5 \approx 6.144, \quad x_6 \approx 6.141, \quad x_7 \approx 6.140\]
소수점 둘째 자리 수렴 → \(x \approx 6.14\)
Step 5 — f(x)로 검증 ★★★
\[f(6.14) = (6.14)^2 - 5(6.14) - 7 \approx -0.0004 < 0\] \[f(6.15) = (6.15)^2 - 5(6.15) - 7 \approx 0.073 > 0\] \[f(6.14) \cdot f(6.15) < 0 \quad \checkmark \quad \therefore\; 6.14 < x < 6.15\]












ii. \(2 - x = \tan x \quad (-\pi/2 < x < \pi/2)\)
Step 1 — f(x) 찾기
\[f(x) = \tan x + x - 2\]
Step 2 — rough range 찾기



\(x\)
\(f(x)\)




0
\(-2 < 0\)


1
\(\tan 1 + 1 - 2 \approx 0.557 > 0\)



\[f(0) \cdot f(1) < 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 0 < x < 1\]
Step 3 — x = g(x) 찾기
\[\tan x = 2 - x \;\Rightarrow\; x = \arctan(2 - x)\]
\[g'(x) = \frac{-1}{1+(2-x)^2}, \quad |g'(x)| \leq \frac{1}{1+1} = 0.5 < 1 \quad \checkmark\]
Step 4 — Iteration (초기값 \(x_0 = 0.5\))
\[x_1 \approx 0.9828, \quad x_2 \approx 0.7954, \quad x_3 \approx 0.8745\] \[x_4 \approx 0.8439, \quad x_5 \approx 0.8560, \quad x_6 \approx 0.8513, \quad x_7 \approx 0.8532\]
소수점 둘째 자리 수렴 → \(x \approx 0.85\)
Step 5 — f(x)로 검증 ★★★
\[f(0.85) = \tan(0.85) + 0.85 - 2 \approx -0.012 < 0\] \[f(0.86) = \tan(0.86) + 0.86 - 2 \approx 0.022 > 0\] \[f(0.85) \cdot f(0.86) < 0 \quad \checkmark \quad \therefore\; 0.85 < x < 0.86\]












iii. \(xe^x = 2\)
Step 1 — f(x) 찾기
\[f(x) = xe^x - 2\]
Step 2 — rough range 찾기



\(x\)
\(f(x)\)




0
\(-2 < 0\)


1
\(e - 2 \approx 0.718 > 0\)



\(f(0) \cdot f(1) < 0 \Rightarrow 0 < x < 1\). 더 좁히면:



\(x\)
\(f(x)\)




0.8
\(0.8 e^{0.8} - 2 \approx -0.220 < 0\)


0.9
\(0.9 e^{0.9} - 2 \approx 0.214 > 0\)



\[\Rightarrow \quad 0.8 < x < 0.9\]
Step 3 — x = g(x) 찾기
\[xe^x = 2 \;\Rightarrow\; x = 2e^{-x}\]
\[g'(x) = -2e^{-x}, \quad |g'(0.8)| = 2e^{-0.8} \approx 0.899 < 1 \quad \checkmark\]
Step 4 — Iteration (초기값 \(x_0 = 0.85\))
\[x_1 \approx 0.8549, \quad x_2 \approx 0.8526, \quad x_3 \approx 0.8536\] \[x_4 \approx 0.8532, \quad x_5 \approx 0.8530, \quad x_6 \approx 0.8530\]
소수점 셋째 자리 수렴 → \(x \approx 0.853\)
Step 5 — f(x)로 검증 ★★★
\[f(0.852) = 0.852\, e^{0.852} - 2 \approx -0.003 < 0\] \[f(0.853) = 0.853\, e^{0.853} - 2 \approx 0.002 > 0\] \[f(0.852) \cdot f(0.853) < 0 \quad \checkmark \quad \therefore\; 0.852 < x < 0.853\]

text:

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Question 1









Figure 1 shows the curve \(y = f(x)\), which has a maximum point at \((-3,\,2)\) and a minimum point at \((2,\,-4)\).
(a) Showing the coordinates of any stationary points, sketch on separate diagrams the graphs of

(i) \(y = f(|x|)\) (4)
(ii) \(y = 3f(2x)\) (3)

(b) Write down the values of the constants \(a\) and \(b\) such that the curve \(y = a + f(x+b)\) has a minimum point at the origin \(O\). (2)

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Question 2
(a) Sketch the graph of \(y = 2 + \sec\!\left(x - \dfrac{\pi}{6}\right)\) for \(x\) in the interval \(0 \le x \le 2\pi\).
Show on your sketch the coordinates of any turning points and the equations of any asymptotes. (5)
(b) Find, in terms of \(\pi\), the \(x\)-coordinates of the points where the graph crosses the \(x\)-axis. (5)

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Q1 (a)(i) — \(y = f(|x|)\) [4 marks]
















Mark
Criteria




M1
Right half (\(x \ge 0\)) identical to original \(f(x)\)


A1
Minimum at \((2,\,-4)\) correctly labelled


M1
Left half is a reflection of the right half in the \(y\)-axis


A1
Minimum at \((-2,\,-4)\) correctly labelled; original \((-3,\,2)\) max removed




Key idea: \(f(|x|)\) replaces the entire left half of \(f(x)\) with the mirror image of the right half. The original maximum at \((-3,2)\) disappears.

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Q1 (a)(ii) — \(y = 3f(2x)\) [3 marks]
















Mark
Criteria




M1
Horizontal stretch by factor \(\tfrac{1}{2}\): \(x\)-coordinates halved


A1
Maximum at \(\left(-\tfrac{3}{2},\,6\right)\) correctly labelled


A1
Minimum at \((1,\,-12)\) correctly labelled




Transformation: \(f(x) \to f(2x)\) compresses horizontally by \(\tfrac{1}{2}\); then \(\times 3\) stretches vertically by \(3\).
\[(-3,\,2) \xrightarrow{\div 2 \text{ on }x} \left(-\tfrac{3}{2},\,2\right) \xrightarrow{\times 3 \text{ on }y} \left(-\tfrac{3}{2},\,6\right)\] \[(2,\,-4) \xrightarrow{\div 2 \text{ on }x} (1,\,-4) \xrightarrow{\times 3 \text{ on }y} (1,\,-12)\]

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Q1 (b) — Values of \(a\) and \(b\) [2 marks]
Required: \(y = a + f(x+b)\) has minimum at the origin \((0,\,0)\).

\(f(x+b)\) translates \(f(x)\) left by \(b\). The minimum \((2,\,-4)\) moves to \((2-b,\,-4)\).
Adding \(a\) shifts the graph up by \(a\). The minimum becomes \((2-b,\,-4+a)\).

Setting this equal to \((0,\,0)\):
\[2 - b = 0 \implies \boxed{b = 2}\] \[-4 + a = 0 \implies \boxed{a = 4}\]



Mark
Criteria




B1
\(b = 2\)


B1
\(a = 4\)

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Q2 (a) — Sketch of \(y = 2 + \sec\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right)\) [5 marks]
















Mark
Criteria




M1
Correct shape: two branches of \(\sec\) with a minimum-type branch in \([0, \tfrac{2\pi}{3})\)


A1
Vertical asymptotes \(x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3}\) and \(x = \tfrac{5\pi}{3}\) labelled


A1
Turning point \(\left(\tfrac{\pi}{6},\,3\right)\) labelled


A1
Turning point \(\left(\tfrac{7\pi}{6},\,1\right)\) labelled


A1
Correct overall shape across all three branches within \([0,2\pi]\)



Derivation of key features:

Asymptotes: \(\cos\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right)=0 \implies x-\tfrac{\pi}{6} = \tfrac{\pi}{2}+n\pi\)
\[x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3} \quad (n=0), \qquad x = \tfrac{5\pi}{3} \quad (n=1)\]
Turning points where \(\tan\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right)=0\), i.e. \(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}=n\pi\):
\[x=\tfrac{\pi}{6}: \quad y = 2+\sec(0) = 3 \quad \longrightarrow \quad \left(\tfrac{\pi}{6},\,3\right)\] \[x=\tfrac{7\pi}{6}: \quad y = 2+\sec(\pi) = 2+(-1) = 1 \quad \longrightarrow \quad \left(\tfrac{7\pi}{6},\,1\right)\]

title: Transformations — Practice Questions
text:
Q2 (b) — \(x\)-intercepts [5 marks]
Set \(y = 0\):
\[2 + \sec\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = 0 \implies \sec\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = -2 \implies \cos\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = -\tfrac{1}{2}\]
Let \(\theta = x - \tfrac{\pi}{6}\). Then \(\cos\theta = -\tfrac{1}{2}\).
\[\theta = \tfrac{2\pi}{3} \quad \text{or} \quad \theta = \tfrac{4\pi}{3} \qquad (\text{principal values in }[0,2\pi])\]
\[x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3} + \tfrac{\pi}{6} = \tfrac{5\pi}{6} \qquad \text{and} \qquad x = \tfrac{4\pi}{3} + \tfrac{\pi}{6} = \tfrac{3\pi}{2}\]
\[\boxed{x = \dfrac{5\pi}{6} \qquad \text{and} \qquad x = \dfrac{3\pi}{2}}\]







Mark
Criteria




M1
Sets \(y=0\) and reaches \(\sec(\cdots) = -2\)


M1
Converts to \(\cos\!\left(x-\tfrac{\pi}{6}\right) = -\tfrac{1}{2}\)


A1
Correct principal value \(\theta = \tfrac{2\pi}{3}\)


A1
Second value \(\theta = \tfrac{4\pi}{3}\)


A1
Both final answers \(x = \tfrac{5\pi}{6}\) and \(x = \tfrac{3\pi}{2}\)

title: Vectors — Core Concepts
text:
\[\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\cos\theta\]






TipKEY




\(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = \vec{b} \cdot \vec{a}\) (commutative) and \(\vec{a} \cdot (\vec{b} + \vec{c}) = \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} + \vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}\) (distributive)
\(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0\) if \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) are perpendicular
\(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\) if \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) are parallel; in particular, \(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a} = |\vec{a}|^2\)



Unit vectors \(i, j, k\):
\[i \cdot i = 1, \qquad i \cdot j = 0\]
Column matrix form:
\[\begin{pmatrix}a_1\\a_2\\a_3\end{pmatrix} \cdot \begin{pmatrix}b_1\\b_2\\b_3\end{pmatrix} = a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3\]






TipKEY — Finding the angle



\[\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\cos\theta = a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3\]
\[\cos\theta = \frac{a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3}{|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|}\]





Ex 1 — Angle between two vectors

Given \(\vec{a} = -i+j+3k\) and \(\vec{b} = 7i-2j+2k\), find the angle between \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\).
\[\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = (-1)(7) + (1)(-2) + (3)(2) = -7 - 2 + 6 = -3\]
\[|\vec{a}| = \sqrt{1+1+9} = \sqrt{11}, \qquad |\vec{b}| = \sqrt{49+4+4} = \sqrt{57}\]
\[\cos\theta = \frac{-3}{\sqrt{11}\cdot\sqrt{57}} = \frac{-3}{\sqrt{627}}\]
\[\boxed{\theta = \arccos\!\left(\frac{-3}{\sqrt{627}}\right) \approx 96.9°}\]




Ex 2 — Perpendicular vectors: find \(\lambda\)

Given \(\vec{a} = 2i-6j+k\) and \(\vec{b} = 5i+2j+\lambda k\) are perpendicular, find \(\lambda\).
If perpendicular: \(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0\)
\[(2)(5) + (-6)(2) + (1)(\lambda) = 0\]
\[10 - 12 + \lambda = 0\]
\[\boxed{\lambda = 2}\]













Equation Type
2D
3D




Vector
\(\vec{r} = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b} = \begin{pmatrix}a_1\\a_2\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}b_1\\b_2\end{pmatrix}\)
\(\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}a_1\\a_2\\a_3\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}b_1\\b_2\\b_3\end{pmatrix}\)


Parametric
\(\begin{cases}x = a_1 + \lambda b_1\\y = a_2 + \lambda b_2\end{cases}\)
\(\begin{cases}x = a_1 + \lambda b_1\\y = a_2 + \lambda b_2\\z = a_3 + \lambda b_3\end{cases}\)


Cartesian
\(\dfrac{x-a_1}{b_1} = \dfrac{y-a_2}{b_2}\)
\(\dfrac{x-a_1}{b_1} = \dfrac{y-a_2}{b_2} = \dfrac{z-a_3}{b_3}\)









NoteNote




\(\vec{a}\): position vector of a point on the line
\(\vec{b}\): direction vector of the line
\(\lambda\): scalar parameter





핵심 아이디어 — 왜 이렇게 쓰나?

직선은 “직선 위의 한 점 + 방향” 만 알면 완전히 결정돼.
\[\vec{r} = \underbrace{\vec{a}}_{\text{직선 위 한 점}} + \lambda\underbrace{\vec{b}}_{\text{방향 벡터}}\]
\(\lambda\)를 바꾸면 직선 위 모든 점을 다 찍을 수 있어. (\(\lambda = 0\)이면 점 \(A\) 자체, \(\lambda = 1\)이면 \(\vec{a}+\vec{b}\), 음수면 반대 방향.)
3가지 표현은 모두 같은 직선이야 — 형태만 다를 뿐:
\[\text{Vector} \xrightarrow{\text{성분별 분리}} \text{Parametric} \xrightarrow{\lambda \text{ 소거}} \text{Cartesian}\]



Vector → Parametric → Cartesian 변환 방법

Step 1. Vector → Parametric (성분별로 분리)
\[\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}3\\2\\-5\end{pmatrix} + t\begin{pmatrix}1\\-6\\-2\end{pmatrix} \implies \begin{cases}x = 3+t\\y = 2-6t\\z = -5-2t\end{cases}\]
Step 2. Parametric → Cartesian (각 식에서 \(\lambda\) 구해 연결)
각 식에서 \(t\)를 표현하면: \(t = x-3\), \(t = \dfrac{y-2}{-6}\), \(t = \dfrac{z+5}{-2}\)
모두 같은 \(t\)이므로:
\[\frac{x-3}{1} = \frac{y-2}{-6} = \frac{z+5}{-2}\]

주의: 방향벡터 성분이 \(0\)이면 \(\dfrac{y-1}{0}\) 꼴이 나오는데, 이는 \(y=1\) (그 좌표가 상수)를 의미해. (Ex 6 참고)





Ex 3 — Line through a point, parallel to a vector

Find a vector equation of the straight line which passes through \(A(3,4,4)\) and is parallel to \((7i-4k)\).
\[\boxed{\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}3\\4\\4\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}7\\0\\-4\end{pmatrix}}\]




Ex 4 — Line through two points

Find a vector equation of the line through \(A(4,5,-1)\) and \(B(6,3,2)\).
Direction vector: \(\overrightarrow{AB} = B - A = \begin{pmatrix}2\\-2\\3\end{pmatrix}\)
\[\boxed{\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}4\\5\\-1\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}2\\-2\\3\end{pmatrix}}\]




Ex 5 — Parametric form from vector equation

Express in parametric form: \(\vec{r} = (3i+2j-5k) + t(i-6j-2k)\)
\[\begin{cases}x = 3 + t\\y = 2 - 6t\\z = -5 - 2t\end{cases}\]




Ex 6 — Cartesian equation from two points

Find a Cartesian equation of the line through \(A(2,1,9)\) and \(B(4,1,-8)\).
Direction vector: \(\overrightarrow{AB} = \begin{pmatrix}2\\0\\-17\end{pmatrix}\)
\[\frac{x-2}{2} = \frac{y-1}{0} = \frac{z-9}{-17}\]
Note: \(\dfrac{y-1}{0}\) means \(y = 1\) (constant — direction component is zero).














Point
Line




Point
① distance between two points



Line
② distance from point to line
③ intersection point, ④ angle between lines, ⑤ distance between skew lines (FP)





\[d = |\vec{b} - \vec{a}| = \sqrt{(b_1-a_1)^2+(b_2-a_2)^2+(b_3-a_3)^2}\]



Set \(l_1 = l_2\) using parameters \(\lambda\) and \(\mu\). Solve the system (2 equations, 2 unknowns). Substitute back to verify the third equation — if inconsistent, lines are skew.



Use direction vectors \(\vec{b}_1\) and \(\vec{b}_2\):
\[\cos\theta = \frac{|\vec{b}_1 \cdot \vec{b}_2|}{|\vec{b}_1||\vec{b}_2|}\]

Take the absolute value to always get the acute angle.




Let \(F\) be the foot of perpendicular from point \(P\) to line \(l: \vec{r} = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b}\).

Write \(F = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b}\) (unknown \(\lambda\))
\(\overrightarrow{PF} \cdot \vec{b} = 0\) (perpendicularity condition) → solve for \(\lambda\)
Find \(F\), then \(d = |\overrightarrow{PF}|\)




Ex 7 — Intersection point of two lines

\[l_1: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}3\\8\\-2\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}2\\-1\\3\end{pmatrix}, \qquad l_2: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}7\\4\\3\end{pmatrix} + \mu\begin{pmatrix}2\\1\\4\end{pmatrix}\]
Setting equal: \(3+2\lambda = 7+2\mu,\; 8-\lambda = 4+\mu,\; -2+3\lambda = 3+4\mu\)
From equations 1 and 2: \(\lambda = 2,\; \mu = -1\).
Check equation 3: \(-2+6 = 4\) and \(3-4 = -1\) — not equal, so lines are skew.




Ex 8 — Acute angle between two lines

\[l_1: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}2\\1\\1\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}3\\-8\\-1\end{pmatrix}, \qquad l_2: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}7\\4\\1\end{pmatrix} + \mu\begin{pmatrix}2\\2\\3\end{pmatrix}\]
\[\vec{b}_1 \cdot \vec{b}_2 = (3)(2)+(-8)(2)+(-1)(3) = 6-16-3 = -13\]
\[|\vec{b}_1| = \sqrt{9+64+1} = \sqrt{74}, \qquad |\vec{b}_2| = \sqrt{4+4+9} = \sqrt{17}\]
\[\cos\theta = \frac{|-13|}{\sqrt{74}\cdot\sqrt{17}} = \frac{13}{\sqrt{1258}}\]
\[\boxed{\theta \approx 68.6°}\]




Ex 9 — Distance from point to line

Find the distance from \(P(4,1,-1)\) to \(l: \vec{r} = (4i-4i+3k) + t(i-2j+2k)\).
Let \(F = \begin{pmatrix}4+t\\-4-2t\\3+2t\end{pmatrix}\). Then \(\overrightarrow{PF} = \begin{pmatrix}t\\-5-2t\\4+2t\end{pmatrix}\).
Perpendicularity: \(\overrightarrow{PF} \cdot \begin{pmatrix}1\\-2\\2\end{pmatrix} = 0\)
\[t + 10 + 4t + 8 + 4t = 0 \implies 9t = -18 \implies t = -2\]
\[F = \begin{pmatrix}2\\0\\-1\end{pmatrix}, \qquad \overrightarrow{PF} = \begin{pmatrix}-2\\-1\\0\end{pmatrix}\]
\[\boxed{d = \sqrt{4+1+0} = \sqrt{5}}\]

title: Vectors — Core Concepts
text:
\[\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\cos\theta\]






TipKEY




\(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = \vec{b} \cdot \vec{a}\) (commutative) and \(\vec{a} \cdot (\vec{b} + \vec{c}) = \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} + \vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}\) (distributive)
\(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0\) if \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) are perpendicular
\(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\) if \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) are parallel; in particular, \(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a} = |\vec{a}|^2\)



Unit vectors \(i, j, k\):
\[i \cdot i = 1, \qquad i \cdot j = 0\]
Column matrix form:
\[\begin{pmatrix}a_1\\a_2\\a_3\end{pmatrix} \cdot \begin{pmatrix}b_1\\b_2\\b_3\end{pmatrix} = a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3\]






TipKEY — Finding the angle



\[\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\cos\theta = a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3\]
\[\cos\theta = \frac{a_1b_1 + a_2b_2 + a_3b_3}{|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|}\]





Ex 1 — Angle between two vectors

Given \(\vec{a} = -i+j+3k\) and \(\vec{b} = 7i-2j+2k\), find the angle between \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\).
\[\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = (-1)(7) + (1)(-2) + (3)(2) = -7 - 2 + 6 = -3\]
\[|\vec{a}| = \sqrt{1+1+9} = \sqrt{11}, \qquad |\vec{b}| = \sqrt{49+4+4} = \sqrt{57}\]
\[\cos\theta = \frac{-3}{\sqrt{11}\cdot\sqrt{57}} = \frac{-3}{\sqrt{627}}\]
\[\boxed{\theta = \arccos\!\left(\frac{-3}{\sqrt{627}}\right) \approx 96.9°}\]




Ex 2 — Perpendicular vectors: find \(\lambda\)

Given \(\vec{a} = 2i-6j+k\) and \(\vec{b} = 5i+2j+\lambda k\) are perpendicular, find \(\lambda\).
If perpendicular: \(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b} = 0\)
\[(2)(5) + (-6)(2) + (1)(\lambda) = 0\]
\[10 - 12 + \lambda = 0\]
\[\boxed{\lambda = 2}\]

title: Vectors — Core Concepts
text:
Equation Type
2D
3D




Vector
\(\vec{r} = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b} = \begin{pmatrix}a_1\\a_2\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}b_1\\b_2\end{pmatrix}\)
\(\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}a_1\\a_2\\a_3\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}b_1\\b_2\\b_3\end{pmatrix}\)


Parametric
\(\begin{cases}x = a_1 + \lambda b_1\\y = a_2 + \lambda b_2\end{cases}\)
\(\begin{cases}x = a_1 + \lambda b_1\\y = a_2 + \lambda b_2\\z = a_3 + \lambda b_3\end{cases}\)


Cartesian
\(\dfrac{x-a_1}{b_1} = \dfrac{y-a_2}{b_2}\)
\(\dfrac{x-a_1}{b_1} = \dfrac{y-a_2}{b_2} = \dfrac{z-a_3}{b_3}\)









NoteNote




\(\vec{a}\): position vector of a point on the line
\(\vec{b}\): direction vector of the line
\(\lambda\): scalar parameter





핵심 아이디어 — 왜 이렇게 쓰나?

직선은 “직선 위의 한 점 + 방향” 만 알면 완전히 결정돼.
\[\vec{r} = \underbrace{\vec{a}}_{\text{직선 위 한 점}} + \lambda\underbrace{\vec{b}}_{\text{방향 벡터}}\]
\(\lambda\)를 바꾸면 직선 위 모든 점을 다 찍을 수 있어. (\(\lambda = 0\)이면 점 \(A\) 자체, \(\lambda = 1\)이면 \(\vec{a}+\vec{b}\), 음수면 반대 방향.)
3가지 표현은 모두 같은 직선이야 — 형태만 다를 뿐:
\[\text{Vector} \xrightarrow{\text{성분별 분리}} \text{Parametric} \xrightarrow{\lambda \text{ 소거}} \text{Cartesian}\]



Vector → Parametric → Cartesian 변환 방법

Step 1. Vector → Parametric (성분별로 분리)
\[\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}3\\2\\-5\end{pmatrix} + t\begin{pmatrix}1\\-6\\-2\end{pmatrix} \implies \begin{cases}x = 3+t\\y = 2-6t\\z = -5-2t\end{cases}\]
Step 2. Parametric → Cartesian (각 식에서 \(\lambda\) 구해 연결)
각 식에서 \(t\)를 표현하면: \(t = x-3\), \(t = \dfrac{y-2}{-6}\), \(t = \dfrac{z+5}{-2}\)
모두 같은 \(t\)이므로:
\[\frac{x-3}{1} = \frac{y-2}{-6} = \frac{z+5}{-2}\]

주의: 방향벡터 성분이 \(0\)이면 \(\dfrac{y-1}{0}\) 꼴이 나오는데, 이는 \(y=1\) (그 좌표가 상수)를 의미해. (Ex 6 참고)





Ex 3 — Line through a point, parallel to a vector

Find a vector equation of the straight line which passes through \(A(3,4,4)\) and is parallel to \((7i-4k)\).
\[\boxed{\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}3\\4\\4\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}7\\0\\-4\end{pmatrix}}\]




Ex 4 — Line through two points

Find a vector equation of the line through \(A(4,5,-1)\) and \(B(6,3,2)\).
Direction vector: \(\overrightarrow{AB} = B - A = \begin{pmatrix}2\\-2\\3\end{pmatrix}\)
\[\boxed{\vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}4\\5\\-1\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}2\\-2\\3\end{pmatrix}}\]




Ex 5 — Parametric form from vector equation

Express in parametric form: \(\vec{r} = (3i+2j-5k) + t(i-6j-2k)\)
\[\begin{cases}x = 3 + t\\y = 2 - 6t\\z = -5 - 2t\end{cases}\]




Ex 6 — Cartesian equation from two points

Find a Cartesian equation of the line through \(A(2,1,9)\) and \(B(4,1,-8)\).
Direction vector: \(\overrightarrow{AB} = \begin{pmatrix}2\\0\\-17\end{pmatrix}\)
\[\frac{x-2}{2} = \frac{y-1}{0} = \frac{z-9}{-17}\]
Note: \(\dfrac{y-1}{0}\) means \(y = 1\) (constant — direction component is zero).

Line

Line
② distance from point to line
③ intersection point, ④ angle between lines, ⑤ distance between skew lines (FP)





\[d = |\vec{b} - \vec{a}| = \sqrt{(b_1-a_1)^2+(b_2-a_2)^2+(b_3-a_3)^2}\]



Set \(l_1 = l_2\) using parameters \(\lambda\) and \(\mu\). Solve the system (2 equations, 2 unknowns). Substitute back to verify the third equation — if inconsistent, lines are skew.



Use direction vectors \(\vec{b}_1\) and \(\vec{b}_2\):
\[\cos\theta = \frac{|\vec{b}_1 \cdot \vec{b}_2|}{|\vec{b}_1||\vec{b}_2|}\]

Take the absolute value to always get the acute angle.




Let \(F\) be the foot of perpendicular from point \(P\) to line \(l: \vec{r} = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b}\).

Write \(F = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b}\) (unknown \(\lambda\))
\(\overrightarrow{PF} \cdot \vec{b} = 0\) (perpendicularity condition) → solve for \(\lambda\)
Find \(F\), then \(d = |\overrightarrow{PF}|\)




Ex 7 — Intersection point of two lines

\[l_1: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}3\\8\\-2\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}2\\-1\\3\end{pmatrix}, \qquad l_2: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}7\\4\\3\end{pmatrix} + \mu\begin{pmatrix}2\\1\\4\end{pmatrix}\]
Setting equal: \(3+2\lambda = 7+2\mu,\; 8-\lambda = 4+\mu,\; -2+3\lambda = 3+4\mu\)
From equations 1 and 2: \(\lambda = 2,\; \mu = -1\).
Check equation 3: \(-2+6 = 4\) and \(3-4 = -1\) — not equal, so lines are skew.




Ex 8 — Acute angle between two lines

\[l_1: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}2\\1\\1\end{pmatrix} + \lambda\begin{pmatrix}3\\-8\\-1\end{pmatrix}, \qquad l_2: \vec{r} = \begin{pmatrix}7\\4\\1\end{pmatrix} + \mu\begin{pmatrix}2\\2\\3\end{pmatrix}\]
\[\vec{b}_1 \cdot \vec{b}_2 = (3)(2)+(-8)(2)+(-1)(3) = 6-16-3 = -13\]
\[|\vec{b}_1| = \sqrt{9+64+1} = \sqrt{74}, \qquad |\vec{b}_2| = \sqrt{4+4+9} = \sqrt{17}\]
\[\cos\theta = \frac{|-13|}{\sqrt{74}\cdot\sqrt{17}} = \frac{13}{\sqrt{1258}}\]
\[\boxed{\theta \approx 68.6°}\]




Ex 9 — Distance from point to line

Find the distance from \(P(4,1,-1)\) to \(l: \vec{r} = (4i-4i+3k) + t(i-2j+2k)\).
Let \(F = \begin{pmatrix}4+t\\-4-2t\\3+2t\end{pmatrix}\). Then \(\overrightarrow{PF} = \begin{pmatrix}t\\-5-2t\\4+2t\end{pmatrix}\).
Perpendicularity: \(\overrightarrow{PF} \cdot \begin{pmatrix}1\\-2\\2\end{pmatrix} = 0\)
\[t + 10 + 4t + 8 + 4t = 0 \implies 9t = -18 \implies t = -2\]
\[F = \begin{pmatrix}2\\0\\-1\end{pmatrix}, \qquad \overrightarrow{PF} = \begin{pmatrix}-2\\-1\\0\end{pmatrix}\]
\[\boxed{d = \sqrt{4+1+0} = \sqrt{5}}\]

title: Basic Forms — Roots, Intercepts, and Asymptotes
text:
2. Single Transformation
Each transformation comes in two flavours — vertical (↕) and horizontal (↔︎).








Type
Vertical ↕
Horizontal ↔︎




Translation
\(f(x) \to f(x)+a\)
\(f(x) \to f(x+a)\)


Stretch
\(f(x) \to af(x)\)
\(f(x) \to f(ax)\)


Reflection
\(f(x) \to -f(x)\)
\(f(x) \to f(-x)\)


Modulus
\(f(x) \to \lvert f(x)\rvert\)
\(f(x) \to f(\lvert x\rvert)\)









TipKEY — Modulus as conditional reflection



\[
\lvert f(x)\rvert = \begin{cases} f(x), & \text{if } f(x)\ge 0 \\ -f(x), & \text{if } f(x)<0 \end{cases}
\qquad\text{(reflect the part below the }x\text{-axis upward)}
\] \[
f(\lvert x\rvert) = \begin{cases} f(x), & \text{if } x\ge 0 \\ f(-x), & \text{if } x<0 \end{cases}
\qquad\text{(reflect the right half onto the left half)}
\]




Example 1 — Modulus sketch and intersection
Given \(f(x)=|x|+2\) and \(g(x)=3\):

Sketch \(f(x)\) and \(g(x)\) on the same diagram.
How many intersection points are there?
Hence, solve \(f(x)=g(x)\).










Solution:
\[
|x|+2 = 3 \implies |x|=1 \implies x = \pm 1
\]
There are 2 intersection points: \((-1,\,3)\) and \((1,\,3)\).



Example 2 — Two modulus functions
Given \(f(x)=|x|\) and \(g(x)=|x+1|\):

Sketch \(f(x)\) and \(g(x)\) on the same diagram.
Hence, solve \(f(x)=g(x)\).










Solution:
\[
|x| = |x+1| \implies x^2 = (x+1)^2 \implies 2x+1=0 \implies x = -\tfrac{1}{2}
\]
One intersection point: \(\left(-\tfrac{1}{2},\,\tfrac{1}{2}\right)\).

title: Basic Forms — Roots, Intercepts, and Asymptotes
text:
3. Combination of Transformations






ImportantKEY — Order of operations



Apply transformations in this order:
\[\text{stretch / reflection} \;(\times) \;\longrightarrow\; \text{translation} \;(+) \;\longrightarrow\; \text{modulus}\]




Example 3 — Describe in words
